retrieval_text: Logwood is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 255 works and 452 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Logwood. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Bark, Gum, Seed, Flower, Fruit. Common preparation co-mentions include Extract, Decoction, Tea, Powder, Tincture, Pill. Common condition co-mentions include Diarrhoea or looseness, Dysentery, Cold or catarrh, Vomiting or nausea, Cough, Consumption. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Bleeding caution, Toxicity watch, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.; Cooley's cyclopaedia of practical receipts and collateral information in the arts, manufactures, professions, and trades, including medicine, pharmacy, hygiene, and domestic economy : designed as a comprehensive supplement to the pharmacopoeia and general book of reference for the manufacturer, tradesman, amateur, and heads of families.; Domestic medicine , or, A treatise on the prevention and cure of diseases by regimen and simple medicines : with observations on sea-bathing, and the use of mineral waters, to which is annexed a dispensary for the use of private practitioners; Domestic medicine , or, A treatise on the prevention and cure of diseases by regimen and simple medicines : with observations on sea-bathing, and the use of the mineral waters, to which is annexed a dispensatory for the use of private practitioners.
